title: Flaming Moe

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Episode opening with Homer's signature exclamation
jokes.punchline: The iconic frustrated grunt that's become synonymous with Homer
jokes.explanation: Homer's 'D'oh!' is perhaps the most iconic sitcom catchphrase ever created. Perfect character integration and economy - maximum comedic impact in one syllable.

jokes.text: Yeah, they think they're better than us just because their performance reviews reflect that fact.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about late shift workers thinking they're better
jokes.punchline: Admits their performance reviews actually do reflect that they're better
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-defeating logic - perfectly structured irony where he undermines his own complaint

jokes.text: That's funny, 'cause I found this candy bar in my desk.
jokes.setup: Lenny mentions leaving a candy bar in his desk
jokes.punchline: Carl deadpan reveals he found someone's candy bar
jokes.explanation: Simple workplace theft joke delivered with appropriate deadpan timing

jokes.text: Who? Persia and Mesopotamia.
jokes.setup: Smithers mentions Iran and Iraq fighting
jokes.punchline: Burns refers to them by ancient names, showing his extreme age
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - his ancient age revealed through historical references only he would use

jokes.text: Charles Montgomery Plantagenet... Schicklgruber... Burns.
jokes.setup: Burns signing his full legal name
jokes.punchline: Reveals absurdly pretentious middle names including Hitler's original surname
jokes.explanation: Dark historical reference combined with Burns' aristocratic pretensions - risky but character-appropriate

jokes.text: It is. I know it's hard to see a young buck like me and think of a day when I'm no longer in the pink.
jokes.setup: Burns acknowledging he's writing his will
jokes.punchline: Calls himself a 'young buck' despite being ancient
jokes.explanation: Standard Burns delusion about his age/health - familiar but well-executed

jokes.text: Quickly, now. We're almost through the half hour per day where you're mentally competent. And... ...demented.
jokes.setup: Lawyer rushing Burns through legal document
jokes.punchline: Burns transforms from competent to demented mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Excellent timing gag with Burns' mental state shift happening exactly when predicted

jokes.text: Gary from Gary's Trapdoor Installation and Repair
jokes.setup: Reading Burns' will beneficiaries
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and suspicious business as major beneficiary
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd business name that implies Burns' villainous activities

jokes.text: Smithers, you are the campiest of my aides du camp
jokes.setup: Smithers asking why he's not in the will
jokes.punchline: Burns uses 'camp' pun while referencing Smithers' sexuality
jokes.explanation: Clever multilingual wordplay that acknowledges Smithers' character while staying period-appropriate for Burns

jokes.text: No Steve Woz-ni-ak thee.
jokes.setup: Burns explaining he respects self-made men
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces Wozniak in archaic English style
jokes.explanation: Burns' antiquated speech pattern applied to modern tech figure - good character consistency

jokes.text: In case you didn't hear me, I said I will never see you as my equal. I heard you very clearly, sir. Excellent.
jokes.setup: Burns repeating his insult to Smithers
jokes.punchline: Smithers confirms he heard the insult, Burns is pleased
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Burns-Smithers dynamic - Burns' cruelty and Smithers' masochistic devotion

jokes.text: You, with the Ben Affleck chin and the Matt Damon everything else.
jokes.setup: Bouncer describing someone trying to get into club
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific celebrity feature mashup
jokes.explanation: Clever celebrity reference with unexpected specificity, though not deeply character-driven

jokes.text: This is a nightclub, not a John F. Kennedy cabinet meeting.
jokes.setup: Smithers' formal appearance being rejected
jokes.punchline: Specific historical comparison to JFK era formality
jokes.explanation: Good historical reference that captures Smithers' outdated formal style

jokes.text: My Scotch is a Scotch and water.
jokes.setup: Smithers ordering Scotch and water
jokes.punchline: Moe's bar is so cheap his Scotch is already watered down
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe line - economical delivery that captures his dive bar's low quality

jokes.text: where they drink for fun, instead of here, where, uh, horrible addiction compels you.
jokes.setup: Moe comparing his bar to competition
jokes.punchline: Admits his customers are pathetic addicts, not fun drinkers
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest self-assessment from Moe that's both dark and accurate to his character

jokes.text: No one wants an executive assistant who only works out six hours a day.
jokes.setup: Smithers explaining why he can't get into the trendy club
jokes.punchline: Six hours of daily workout is presented as inadequate
jokes.explanation: Satirizes gym culture excess while showing Smithers' dedication to physical fitness

jokes.text: That was my grandmother's wedding urinal!
jokes.setup: Customer presumably breaks something in the bar
jokes.punchline: Moe's sentimental attachment to a disgusting bathroom fixture
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Moe line combining family sentiment with gross bathroom humor

jokes.text: Look at them renovations! / And... finished!
jokes.setup: Characters excited about bar renovations happening
jokes.punchline: The renovations are instantly completed, implying they were minimal
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about how little actual renovation was needed or done to transform the bar.

jokes.text: Sorry, hobbits. It's back to the shire for you. Slam!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy winning at Scrabble
jokes.punchline: Uses LOTR reference to dismiss opponents
jokes.explanation: Perfectly in-character for Comic Book Guy - nerdy reference delivered with superiority

jokes.text: Isn't that the place where all those rats committed suicide?
jokes.setup: Someone pointing out Moe's tavern
jokes.punchline: Even rats found the place so depressing they killed themselves
jokes.explanation: Dark and memorable description of Moe's bar that's both funny and plausible

jokes.text: What about super-hairy Wonder Woman?
jokes.setup: Asking if the new bar policy welcomes different types
jokes.punchline: Describing someone as 'super-hairy Wonder Woman' - presumably Comic Book Guy in drag
jokes.explanation: Visual joke about Comic Book Guy's appearance and the absurdity of his potential drag persona.

jokes.text: There's never any chicks here. Somehow, there's more no chicks here than usual.
jokes.setup: Barney noticing something different about Moe's
jokes.punchline: Paradoxical concept of degrees of absence
jokes.explanation: Cleverly absurd logic about measuring absence - mathematically impossible but somehow makes sense

jokes.text: He's washing his hands. I'm never gonna get that image out of my head.
jokes.setup: Homer asking what someone's doing in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Traumatized by basic hygiene
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - disgusted by normal behavior, reveals his own poor hygiene

jokes.text: Your standard markup is 400%?
jokes.setup: Homer looking at bar pricing
jokes.punchline: Outrageous but probably realistic bar markup percentage
jokes.explanation: Standard observation about bar pricing - realistic but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Don't you dare question the gospel according to Dr. Swig McJigger.
jokes.setup: Homer questioning bar prices
jokes.punchline: Moe invents ridiculous authority figure for bar pricing
jokes.explanation: Good made-up name with drinking puns, delivered with appropriate fake authority

jokes.text: Total disaster. A complete bomb. Utter failure. Uh, dream I painted.
jokes.setup: Moe listing his past bar concepts
jokes.punchline: Adding 'dream I painted' as if it's another type of failed bar theme
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection where Moe lists failures then adds something that doesn't belong in the category.

jokes.text: Come on, pretend he meant something to you.
jokes.setup: Music teacher announcing his departure
jokes.punchline: Lisa has to coach classmates to fake caring
jokes.explanation: Realistic school moment - Lisa's social awareness contrasted with classmates' indifference

jokes.text: I'm not the soul mate! I'm dating one of your mothers. I-I forget whose.
jokes.setup: Largo introducing his supposed soul mate
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's dating a student's mother but doesn't remember which one
jokes.explanation: Appropriately awkward teacher moment with good cringe factor

jokes.text: ...the scent of music is on you forever!
jokes.setup: New age teacher explaining her teaching philosophy
jokes.punchline: Treating music like a physical substance you can smell
jokes.explanation: Good new-age parody with appropriately ridiculous hippie logic

jokes.text: Tick-tock, Dewey! Tanglewood beckons! / Coming, Dewey! / We're both named Dewey.
jokes.setup: One Dewey calling to another
jokes.punchline: Revealing that both partners in this relationship are named Dewey
jokes.explanation: Simple absurd detail that adds to the quirkiness of these characters and their relationship.

jokes.text: I am not Comic Book Guy. I am his cousin, Comic Book Gay.
jokes.setup: Character being mistaken for Comic Book Guy
jokes.punchline: Related character with parallel naming convention
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character naming joke with clear family resemblance

jokes.text: What do you mean, wig?
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on his wig
jokes.punchline: Character's genuine confusion about obvious toupee
jokes.explanation: Standard wig denial joke - visual gag that relies on obvious bad hairpiece

jokes.text: Doctor already did.
jokes.setup: Someone threatening to 'knock him inside out'
jokes.punchline: References gender reassignment surgery
jokes.explanation: Quick, dark comeback with efficient wordplay on 'inside out'

jokes.text: the Tony Awards are that night, and I'll be too keyed up to have fun.
jokes.setup: Moe being invited to Gay Day at Krustyland
jokes.punchline: Moe pretending to be too excited about the Tony Awards to go out
jokes.explanation: Moe trying to maintain his gay persona by referencing theater awards he clearly knows nothing about.

jokes.text: Oh, I love you, Neil Patrick Hairless.
jokes.setup: Character expressing affection
jokes.punchline: Replaces 'Harris' with 'Hairless' referencing baldness
jokes.explanation: Simple celebrity name pun - functional but not particularly inspired

jokes.text: I was gonna have to move in with Ma, who's dead and doesn't have a house.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining his desperate financial situation
jokes.punchline: Logically impossible backup plan involving dead mother with no house
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe line - dark humor about family combined with impossible logic delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: I am Angel Moe.
jokes.setup: Moe considering telling the truth about his sexuality
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by imaginary angel/devil scenario
jokes.explanation: Standard angel/devil on shoulder trope - serves plot but not particularly funny

jokes.text: everybody vogue!
jokes.setup: Moe about to come clean to his gay patrons
jokes.punchline: Instead deflecting by starting a dance party
jokes.explanation: Moe's avoidance tactic using gay culture references he's picked up, avoiding the difficult conversation.

jokes.text: You misfiled the... permission slips. You keep the green. I need the white.
jokes.setup: Skinner getting distracted by attractive teacher
jokes.punchline: Reverts to obsessive bureaucratic behavior in romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment - his bureaucratic obsessions override even romantic attraction

jokes.text: Hey, come back to Earth, Mr. Permission Man. / You're all right.
jokes.setup: Ms. Juniper calling out Skinner's obsessiveness
jokes.punchline: Skinner taking this as a compliment about being 'all right'
jokes.explanation: Skinner's desperate interpretation of mild criticism as romantic validation shows his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Ooh, a splendid gentlemen's club. You've earned my respect.
jokes.setup: Burns visiting Moe's transformed bar
jokes.punchline: Burns completely misunderstanding what kind of establishment it is
jokes.explanation: Burns' old-fashioned terminology creates confusion about what type of 'gentlemen's club' he thinks he's seeing.

jokes.text: Hey, Smithers, I didn't know you were a geezer-pleaser.
jokes.setup: Moe seeing Smithers with Burns at the gay bar
jokes.punchline: Creates rhyming term for someone who dates elderly men
jokes.explanation: Clever rhyming slang that captures the age gap dynamic

jokes.text: Having a 'lemon party'? Ooh, a good old-fashioned lemon party. I call first squeeze.
jokes.setup: Burns hearing about a 'lemon party'
jokes.punchline: Innocent interpretation of sexual slang term
jokes.explanation: Adult humor disguised as innocent misunderstanding - risky but character-appropriate for Burns' age

jokes.text: tonight I've got a long-overdue manscaping.
jokes.setup: Moe declining the Grease invitation
jokes.punchline: His excuse involves personal grooming he wouldn't normally discuss
jokes.explanation: Moe trying to sound gay-culturally appropriate with TMI about personal grooming.

jokes.text: Now we can afford real bowls of pretzels instead of trompe l'oeiling them on the bar.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining how the business success has improved things
jokes.punchline: Revealing he used to paint fake pretzels on the bar instead of providing real ones
jokes.explanation: Exceptional absurdist humor showing extreme cheapness through art technique terminology. Perfect Moe characterization.

jokes.text: A Tom of Shelbyville?
jokes.setup: Moe putting up homoerotic artwork
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'Tom of Finland' gay art reference with Simpsons geography
jokes.explanation: Clever art world reference adapted to Simpsons universe - good cultural knowledge required

jokes.text: instead of trompe l'oeiling them on the bar.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining bar improvements
jokes.punchline: Uses sophisticated art term for painting fake pretzels
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of highbrow art terminology with Moe's cheap bar tricks

jokes.text: That wasn't me and you're my alibi.
jokes.setup: Skinner asking what Fat Tony is doing at school
jokes.punchline: Immediately establishes alibi before crime is revealed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fat Tony line - preemptively criminal thinking delivered with casual authority

jokes.text: Triple spring break plus four weeks at Christmas. / No, but... no, if, if the state finds out, they'll shut us down.
jokes.setup: Bart negotiating for more vacation time
jokes.punchline: Skinner worried the excessive vacation will get the school shut down
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how desperate Skinner is and how Bart takes advantage of authority figures.

jokes.text: You get a booty call and I get a cootie call.
jokes.setup: Skinner's dating scheme involving children
jokes.punchline: Bart creates parallel between adult dating and child interaction
jokes.explanation: Clever rhyming wordplay that captures the unfairness of Skinner's proposal

jokes.text: I've sketched you so many times in my dream journal.
jokes.setup: Melody expressing excitement about meeting Bart
jokes.punchline: Reveals obsessive behavior about drawing him
jokes.explanation: Appropriately creepy child stalker behavior - uncomfortable but age-appropriate

jokes.text: Bart rides that board with a passion of an Arapaho ghost dancer.
jokes.setup: Ms. Juniper watching Bart skateboard
jokes.punchline: Her overly spiritual and culturally inappropriate comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Ms. Juniper's inappropriate new-age spirituality and cultural insensitivity.

jokes.text: Words are your enemy, my love.
jokes.setup: Skinner asking why she likes him
jokes.punchline: Tells him to stop talking because he ruins everything he says
jokes.explanation: Perfect advice for Skinner's character - his overthinking ruins romantic moments

jokes.text: This morning I was like... ♪ Ding-a-ling-a-ling. ♪ and now I'm like... ♪ Dong-dong-dong. ♪
jokes.setup: Skinner describing his emotional transformation
jokes.punchline: Uses bell sounds as metaphor for arousal progression
jokes.explanation: Appropriately awkward Skinner attempt at expressing sexuality through sound effects

jokes.text: This morning I was like... ♪ Ding-a-ling-a-ling. ♪ and now I'm like... ♪ Dong-dong-dong. ♪
jokes.setup: Bart describing his emotional change
jokes.punchline: Using sound effects to represent going from happy to depressed
jokes.explanation: Creative way for Bart to express emotions through sound effects rather than words.

jokes.text: Will you become Springfield's first openly gay city councilman?
jokes.setup: Gay community asking Moe to represent them politically
jokes.punchline: Want him to be 'openly gay' when he's actually straight
jokes.explanation: Good irony about representation - they want honesty from someone who's lying to them

jokes.text: I thought it was an inventory issue. That's a big relief.
jokes.setup: Smithers asking to discuss something serious in private
jokes.punchline: Moe more worried about bar inventory than sexuality crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe priorities - business concerns outweigh personal identity crisis

jokes.text: ♪ I'm walking on sunshine ♪ ♪ Not literally ♪ ♪ It means that I'm happy post-coitally ♪
jokes.setup: Skinner singing happily after his date
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining the metaphor and being inappropriately explicit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character moment - his pedantic nature ruins his own romantic song.

jokes.text: You're a regular Casanova. Or in English, 'new house.'
jokes.setup: Chalmers complimenting Skinner's romantic success
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstanding what 'Casanova' means
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Chalmers malapropism showing his educational incompetence through false etymology.

jokes.text: You're breaking up with me... upside down?
jokes.setup: Bart doing handstand while breaking up
jokes.punchline: Focuses on physical position rather than emotional content
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup with child-appropriate relationship drama

jokes.text: Raggedy Ann was so right about you.
jokes.setup: Melody being disappointed in Bart
jokes.punchline: Referencing advice from her doll as if it were a real person
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Melody's childish mentality through her relationship with toys.

jokes.text: Think of me whenever you see a tattoo you don't understand.
jokes.setup: Ms. Juniper saying goodbye to Skinner
jokes.punchline: Romantic farewell tied to incomprehensible body art
jokes.explanation: Perfect hippie character line - connects emotion to random cultural observation

jokes.text: Think of me whenever you see a tattoo you don't understand.
jokes.setup: Ms. Juniper saying goodbye to Skinner
jokes.punchline: Her oddly specific and nonsensical way to be remembered
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ms. Juniper line - appropriately random, spiritual, and nonsensical for her character.

jokes.text: Fibs break baby Jesus's ribs.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Skinner catching Skinner in a lie about movies
jokes.punchline: Creates rhyming moral lesson about lying
jokes.explanation: Excellent Mrs. Skinner line - religious guilt with perfect rhyme scheme and absurd imagery

jokes.text: Who's the new groundskeeper? Also you, and you don't get more money.
jokes.setup: Willie asking about groundskeeper position after promotion
jokes.punchline: Gets both jobs but no pay increase
jokes.explanation: Good workplace humor about promotion without benefits - realistic and cruel

jokes.text: Willie, you're principal now. / Really? / Who's the new groundskeeper? / Also you, and you don't get more money. / Ach. Yes.
jokes.setup: Skinner leaving and promoting Willie
jokes.punchline: Willie getting double the work for the same pay and accepting it
jokes.explanation: Good workplace humor about exploitation with Willie's characteristic resigned acceptance.

jokes.text: My name is Moe Szyslak, and I'm here to elect me.
jokes.setup: Moe giving a campaign speech
jokes.punchline: His grammatically incorrect and self-centered opening
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe butchering of formal speech, showing his lack of education and self-awareness.

jokes.text: I only read Hustler to see the pictures of Larry Flynt.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to prove he's gay
jokes.punchline: Claims to read porn magazine for publisher photos instead of centerfolds
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of 'reading it for the articles' cliché with wheelchair-bound publisher

jokes.text: I only read Hustler to see the pictures of Larry Flynt.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to prove he's gay
jokes.punchline: Claiming attraction to the magazine's publisher rather than the models
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic from Moe trying to prove homosexuality through attraction to an unlikely figure.

jokes.text: Most insidious stereotype ever.
jokes.setup: Moe claiming all gay men are attracted to all men
jokes.punchline: Character directly calls out harmful stereotype
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary that acknowledges and corrects harmful assumption while staying in character

jokes.text: At first, I was only gay for your money.
jokes.setup: Moe confessing his deception to the gay community
jokes.punchline: Treats sexuality as transactional business decision
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe logic - reduces complex identity to simple financial motivation with typical honesty

jokes.text: I just wish you people could know what it's like to want acceptance.
jokes.setup: Moe apologizing to the gay community
jokes.punchline: Telling an oppressed minority that they don't know what it's like to want acceptance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic statement showing Moe's complete lack of awareness about the struggles of the very people he's addressing.

jokes.text: Well, so who you gonna vote for, me, or a Republican whose record is so anti-gay, he's clearly secretly super gay.
jokes.setup: Moe asking for votes despite his deception
jokes.punchline: Describing the classic closeted politician stereotype
jokes.explanation: Standard political satire about closeted anti-gay politicians, not particularly original but competently executed.

jokes.text: the liar or the denier.
jokes.setup: Describing election choice between Moe and Republican opponent
jokes.punchline: Parallel construction about different types of dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Good political commentary with efficient parallel phrasing about electoral choices

jokes.text: Must not the heavens themselves cry with rage?
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy responding to political disappointment
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top dramatic language for a minor setback
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy melodrama, treating minor inconvenience as cosmic injustice.

jokes.text: Like Frisbee golf, I'm glad I tried it once.
jokes.setup: Moe reflecting on kissing a man
jokes.punchline: Compares homosexual experience to niche recreational activity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward Moe comparison that's both dismissive and oddly respectful

jokes.text: You've been gone for three months. Groovy.
jokes.setup: Skinner saying it could have been a day or lifetime
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was gone much longer than expected, hippie response
jokes.explanation: Good time distortion joke with appropriate hippie nonchalance about extended absence

jokes.text: Puttin' everything back. Undo. Undo. Undo. Sports page back over the urinal, and done.
jokes.setup: Moe restoring his bar to its original state
jokes.punchline: The final touch is putting newspaper back over the urinal
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Moe's disgusting bar conditions with the sports page serving as urinal decoration.

tmdb_overview: When Smithers transforms Moe's pub into a refined and trendy lounge, the establishment becomes Springfield's local gay hangout; Principal Skinner falls for the new, free-spirited music teacher.
